description: §20.8.1 — `<outlet>` is the `<program>` shell's route-content swap region; an `<outlet>` with no `<program>` ancestor (here nested in a plain `<div>` at file top level) fires E-OUTLET-OUTSIDE-SHELL. Unlike E-CHANNEL-OUTSIDE-PROGRAM there is no module-file dispensation — an outlet has no meaning without a shell.
rationale: §20.8.1: 'an `<outlet>` outside a `<program>` shell SHALL be E-OUTLET-OUTSIDE-SHELL.' Outside a shell there is no route content to swap in.
